title: Seismic structure of the north-central Chilean convergent margin: Subduction erosion of a paleomagmatic arc
abstract: A joint refraction and reflection travel time tomographic approach derives a 2-D velocity-depth model for the north-central Chilean margin at ~31°S. The outermost fore-arc block is composed of volcanic rocks that are largely hydrofractured as a consequence of subduction erosion. The landward limit of this block coincides with a continental slope scarp suggesting tectonic subsidence.

location.description: Offshore north-central Chile at ~31°S, Juan Fernández Ridge collision zone transition area

seismic_lines.name: SPOC Profile offshore Punitaqui (~31°S) — 26 OBH/OBS, ~196 km, SO161 Legs 1&4

data.name: Wide-Angle OBS/OBH Refraction Tomography — North-central Chile ~31°S
data.description: Wide-angle P-wave OBS/OBH refraction data from ~31°S used in joint refraction-reflection tomography; reveals paleomagmatic arc basement hydrofractured by subduction erosion

data.name: MCS Reflection Profile — North-central Chile ~31°S
data.description: Multichannel seismic reflection data used as constraints on the joint tomographic inversion at ~31°S

data.name: Free-air Gravity — North-central Chile ~31°S
data.description: Shipboard gravity data used to supplement velocity model at the north-central Chile margin

_data_note: Specific cruise not definitively identified from available metadata. Likely related to SO213 or SO207 GEOMAR cruises targeting the central Chilean margin around 2011.

processing.summary: Joint refraction and reflection travel-time tomography applied to wide-angle OBS/OBH seismic data from a single trench-perpendicular profile at ~31°S to derive a 2D Vp model of the north-central Chilean convergent margin. The velocity model reveals the geometry of the subducting Nazca plate, a hydrofractured paleomagmatic arc forearc block, and a continental slope scarp marking the limit of tectonic subsidence.
workflow: 1. OBS/OBH data processing and instrument relocation
processing.notes: Short GRL paper; high-confidence velocity model at 31°S fills gap between well-studied CINCA (~23°S) and CONDOR (~32°S) transects

analysis_notes: Validated against PDF. year_acquired 2011→2001 (data acquired 2000/2001 onboard R/V Sonne). Vessel updated to SO161 SPOC Legs 1 & 4 (confirmed from ref: Flueh and Kopp 2002, Geomar Rep. 102). source_volume_cui=3124 (20 airguns, 51.2 L). 26 OBH/OBS, ~196 km profile offshore Punitaqui. Shot interval 60 s, 150 m spacing. Software: TOMO2D confirmed (Korenaga et al. 2000). FONDECYT 1130004. Data not in public repository (Reichert et al. 2002 = SO161 Legs 2&3 SPOC south; this is Legs 1&4 north Chile).
